rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA LAVIN et al VS. ST. HELENA HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 23, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANTS ADVENTIST HEALTH CALIFORNIA MEDICAL GROUP, INC. AND BRIAN STEBER, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO MEDICAL RECORDS AND DR. SMITH'S DECLARATION SUSTAINED. DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. (SEE GARIBAY V. HEMMAT (2008) 161 CAL.APP.4TH 735.) THE COURT DENIES FOR DUE PROCESS REASONS THE REQUEST TO ENTERTAIN THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED ON REPLY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND VS. ONVOI BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 23, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT ONVOI BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE COURT RULES UPON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE AS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS THE SAME FACTS AND DATES FOR THE FRAUD ARE ALLEGED IN BOTH THE ORIGINAL AND AMENDED COMPLAINTS. THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE NOVEMBER 18, 2003 LETTER SENT BY REGAN ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF KNEW OF DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED FRAUD AT THAT TIME. THE LETTER STATES THAT DEFENDANT "MATERIALLY MISREPRESENTED" CLAIMS WITH RESPECT TO ITS CLIENT, SELECT. (SEE HANSON DECLARATION.) THE LETTER IS UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ON NOTICE AND A "REASONABLE PERSON" WOULD HAVE KNOWN TO INVESTIGATE FURTHER. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STATUTORY PENALTY WHICH IS ALSO TIME-BARRED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN C MCKEAN et al VS. STEPHEN E LAWRENCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7 Against Defendats Stephen Lawrence, Sophie Gasparatos, And Their Attorney Russ Martin
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 23, 2009, line 14, PLAINTIFFS SEAN MCKEAN AND SHAWN MCILVENNA'S Notice Of Request For Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To CCP 128.7 Against Defendants Stephen Lawrence, Sophie Gasparatos, And Their Attorney Russ Martin. PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. CCP SECTION 128.7(b) WAS NOT VIOLATED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION WAS NOT PRESENTED FOR AN IMPROPER PURPOSE, THE CLAIMS WERE WARRANTED BY EXISTING LAW, AND THE MOTION WAS BASED UPON EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS MOTION WAS NOT BROUGHT PRIMARILY FOR AN IMPROPER PURPOSE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ABDULLAH AWADALLA VS. CLAUDIUS OLIVIERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Abdullah Awadalla'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 23, 2009, line 16, Plaintiff Abdullah Awadalla's Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. MOTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON HIS CLAIMS AND THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES AND HARMS WEIGHS IN PLAINTIFF'S FAVOR BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS ARE DESTROYING THE BUSINESS. DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM HANDLING THE RESTAURANT'S MONEY OR OTHERWISE INTERFERING IN THE OPERATION OF THE BUSINESS. DEFENDANT IS TO STAY 50 YARDS AWAY FROM THE RESTAURANT. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS A STAY AWAY ORDER REGARDING INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYEES, APPLICATION MUST BE MADE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 527.6 AND SET ON THE COURT'S HARASSMENT CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT THAT IS EQUAL TO DEFENDANT'S SALARY FOR ONE YEAR. =(302/CWW)
